channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Debate over tennis tournament formats, British player injuries and financial barriers to success
item.description: The discussion centers on the structure of professional tennis tournaments, with debates about whether men's and women's grand slam events should start with best-of-five sets from round one. Critics a...

item.title: White House Condemns Reporter's Air Force One Swap Story as 'Fake News'
item.description: The White House's communications director, Steven Cheung, launched a scathing attack on a Pulitzer-winning reporter, Carol Leonnig, for a story detailing President Donald Trump's abrupt switch from th...

item.title: Pakistan's Record Remittances Reach $41.6 Billion in FY26
item.description: Pakistan received a record $41.6 billion in workers’ remittances during the fiscal year 2025-26, marking an 8.6% increase compared to the previous year. This milestone, according to State Bank of Paki...

item.title: Nigerians Earning N60,000 Better Off Than Some UK Residents, Says Bwala
item.description: Daniel Bwala, President Tinubu's policy communication adviser, warned that many Nigerian migrants in the UK are struggling with low-paying jobs despite their education. He argued that graduates often ...

item.title: The Morning Glory Cloud: A Spectacular Atmospheric Phenomenon in Australia
item.description: The Morning Glory cloud is a rare atmospheric phenomenon that occurs in the southern Gulf of Carpentaria, northern Queensland, Australia. This roll cloud can stretch up to 1,000 kilometers in length, ...

item.title: Abhijeet Dipke Alleges Government Blocked His Video Requesting Tents for Protesters at Jantar Mantar Amid Heavy Rainfall
item.description: In New Delhi, the ongoing protest at Jantar Mantar against alleged examination irregularities has entered its 20th day. Abhijeet Dipke, founder of Cockroach Janta Party, has raised serious concerns af...

item.title: Reli German, key figure in 1986 People Power Revolution, passes away at 86
item.description: Reli German, one of the founders of the August Twenty-One Movement and a pivotal strategist in the 1986 People Power Revolution, has passed away at the age of 86. His granddaughter, Alex Castro, and t...

item.title: RCMP issue Amber Alert for missing 6-year-old boy in Alberta
item.description: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) near the Alberta-British Columbia border have issued an Amber Alert for a six-year-old boy named Lanakai Morrison, who was taken on Tuesday from Valhalla Centr...

item.title: Linda Mwananchi accuses Ruto of political violence and state intimidation in Kenya
item.description: The Linda Mwananchi movement has accused President William Ruto's administration of fostering a culture of violence and intimidation, claiming Kenya is regressing to authoritarian practices. In a Stat...

item.title: Zimbabwe-born man suspected of murdering family in UK flees to Zimbabwe
item.description: British police have launched an international manhunt for Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, a 45-year-old British citizen of Zimbabwean heritage, following the discovery of his wife and two daughters' bodies ...

item.title: TFRA takes strict action against fertilizer traders violating subsidy system
item.description: Tanzania’s Fertilizer Regulatory Authority (TFRA) has intensified enforcement measures to combat traders selling subsidized fertilizers outside the official digital platform or at inflated prices. A c...

item.title: Right-Wing Influencers Clash Over Conspiracy Theories and Personal Attacks

item.title: A Look Back at Kelly McGillis' Career and Iconic Screen Presence Through the 1980s
item.description: This retrospective article celebrates actress Kelly McGillis and her rise to prominence during the 1980s, highlighting the performances and qualities that established her as one of Hollywood's most re...

item.title: Comelec reduces voters per precinct to prevent long lines in Bangsamoro elections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has implemented measures to ensure shorter voting lines and faster ballot processing during the Bangsamoro parliamentary elections on September 14. Comelec Chair ...

item.title: ACT-Wazalendo and CCM Commit to Political Reconciliation for Zanzibar's Development
item.description: The chairman of ACT-Wazalendo, Othman Masoud Othman, has assured the public that the ongoing political reconciliation process in Zanzibar is aimed at serving the broader interests of residents rather ...

item.title: Baruipur police encounter: Family rejects body, opposition condemns action
item.description: The last rites of Prabhas Mondal, one of the accused in the Baruipur rape and murder case, were conducted by local authorities after his family refused to claim his body. Mondal was killed in a police...

item.title: Bengaluru removes 430 km of footpath encroachments, to issue ID cards to street vendors
item.description: The Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA) has cleared 430 km of footpath encroachments across the city between July 1 and 8, 2026, as part of its initiative to reclaim pedestrian spaces. The government as...

item.title: Ontario educators discuss key challenges in education policy
item.description: The article highlights an upcoming live discussion where Ontario teachers will address pressing educational concerns. Following student feedback on classroom issues like class sizes and special educat...

item.title: Kenya Lawyer Released After Arrest Over Stalled Vehicle, Alleged Political Motives
item.description: Lawyer Ndegwa Njiru, a key ally of DCP Leader Rigathi Gachagua, was released from police custody after being arrested following his vehicle's breakdown on Thika Highway. Njiru claims officers from Kas...

item.title: Yoruba-Hausa clash in Ibadan leaves four dead; Oyo State orders illegal structures removal
item.description: A violent clash between Yoruba traders and Hausa scavengers in Ibadan's Ojoo area left four people dead, prompting Oyo State to deploy security forces and order the removal of illegal structures. The ...
